prompt: How many member states does the United Nations have?
note: 193 UN member states. People guess high — they're counting territories and observers like the Vatican and Palestine.

prompt: How many bones are in the adult human body?
note: 206 in adults. Babies start with about 270 that fuse together as they grow.

prompt: How tall is Mount Everest above sea level, in metres?
note: 8,849 m — just under 9 km, or about 5.5 miles straight up.

prompt: How many keys are on a standard piano?
note: 88 keys — 52 white, 36 black, a bit over seven octaves.

prompt: How many elements are on the periodic table?
note: 118 confirmed elements, ending (so far) at oganesson.

prompt: What is the population of Canada?
note: About 41 million — roughly one-eighth of the US, despite Canada being larger in area.

prompt: How many Wimbledon men's singles titles did Roger Federer win?
note: 8 — the men's record at Wimbledon.

prompt: What is the speed of light, in kilometres per second?
note: About 299,792 km/s — fast enough to lap the Earth roughly 7.5 times in one second.

prompt: How long is the Great Wall of China, end to end, in kilometres?
note: The 2012 official survey measured all branches at about 21,000 km — far longer than the single wall people picture.

prompt: How many seconds are there in one day?
How many sovereign countries are there in Africa?
note: 54 UN-recognised states. The African Union counts 55, including Western Sahara — a common source of disagreement.

prompt: How many teeth does a typical adult have?
note: 32 including the four wisdom teeth — many adults have those removed and live with 28.

prompt: How long is a marathon, in metres?
note: 42,195 m — fixed in 1921 from the 1908 London course length, not a round number by design.

prompt: At what temperature does water boil, in degrees Fahrenheit?
note: 212 °F at sea level — the Fahrenheit scale's other anchor point, 180 degrees above freezing.

prompt: What is the average distance from the Earth to the Moon, in kilometres?
note: About 384,400 km on average — roughly 30 Earth-diameters of empty space between us.

prompt: What is the average distance from the Earth to the Sun, in kilometres?
note: About 149.6 million km — this distance is defined as one Astronomical Unit (AU).

prompt: How many chromosomes does a human cell normally have?
note: 46 — arranged in 23 pairs, one set from each parent.

prompt: How tall is the Burj Khalifa, in metres?
note: 828 m to the tip — held the 'world's tallest' title since 2010.

prompt: What is the atomic number of gold?
note: 79 — meaning a gold atom has 79 protons. Its symbol Au comes from the Latin 'aurum'.

prompt: How long is the River Nile, in kilometres?
note: About 6,650 km — long considered the world's longest river, though the Amazon is a close rival.

prompt: How many planets are in our solar system?
note: 8 since 2006, when Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet.

prompt: Roughly how many times does an adult heart beat in a day?
note: Around 100,000 — about 60–100 beats a minute, all day, every day.

prompt: How fast does sound travel through air, in metres per second?
note: About 343 m/s at 20 °C — which is why you see lightning before you hear thunder.

prompt: How many squares are on a standard chessboard?
note: 64 — an 8x8 grid of alternating light and dark squares.

prompt: What is the land area of Russia, in square kilometres?
note: About 17.1 million km^2 — the largest country on Earth, nearly twice the size of Canada.

prompt: What is the diameter of the Earth, in kilometres?
note: About 12,742 km on average — slightly wider at the equator than pole-to-pole.

prompt: How many states are in the United States?
note: 50 — the most recent additions were Alaska and Hawaii, both in 1959.

prompt: How many hours are there in a (non-leap) year?
How deep is the deepest point of the ocean (the Mariana Trench), in metres?
note: About 10,935 m at Challenger Deep — deeper than Everest is tall.

prompt: What is the diameter of the Sun, in kilometres?
note: About 1.39 million km — roughly 109 Earths laid side by side.

prompt: How many minutes are there in a week?
note: 60 x 24 x 7 = 10,080 minutes.

prompt: At what temperature does water freeze, in kelvin?
note: 273.15 K = 0 °C. Kelvin starts at absolute zero, so there are no negative temperatures.

prompt: How long is the Amazon River, in kilometres?
note: About 6,400 km — its exact length is debated, with some surveys claiming it beats the Nile.

prompt: How long is the Great Barrier Reef, in kilometres?
note: About 2,300 km — the largest living structure on Earth, visible from space.

prompt: How many bones are in the adult human skull?
note: 22 — eight cranial bones protecting the brain plus fourteen facial bones.

prompt: How fast does the Earth travel around the Sun, in kilometres per second?
note: About 30 km/s — you're hurtling through space far faster than any rocket while sitting still.

prompt: How many member countries are in the European Union?
note: 27 since 2020, when the UK left.

prompt: How many ribs does a typical human have?
note: 24 — twelve pairs. The old myth that men have one fewer than women is false.

prompt: How many minutes are there in a day?
note: 60 x 24 = 1,440 minutes.

prompt: How many holes are played in a standard round of golf?
note: 18 — the figure became standard at St Andrews in the 18th century.

prompt: How many official constellations are recognised by astronomers?
note: 88 — the boundaries were fixed by the International Astronomical Union in 1922.

prompt: Roughly how many bones is a human baby born with?
note: About 270 — many fuse together as a child grows, leaving 206 in adults.

prompt: How many stripes are on the United States flag?
note: 13 — one for each of the original colonies; the 50 stars represent the current states.

prompt: What is the straight-line distance from London to New York, in kilometres?
note: About 5,570 km as the crow flies — actual flight paths curve north over the Atlantic.

prompt: How many seconds are there in a week?
